title: Surrounded Regions | Replace O’s with X’s
content:
Problem Statement: Given a matrix mat of size N x M where every element is either ‘O’ or ‘X’. Replace all ‘O’ with ‘X’ that is surrounded by ‘X’. An ‘O’ (or a set of ‘O’) is considered to be surrounded by ‘X’ if there are ‘X’ at locations just below, just above just left, and just right of it.

Examples 
Example 1:
Input: n = 5, m = 4


Output:




Example 2:
Input: n = 5, m = 4



Output:

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Approach
Algorithm

The problem is about converting all 'O' regions that are completely surrounded by 'X' into 'X'. However, any 'O' that is connected to the boundary cannot be converted, since it's not fully surrounded.

So the key idea is:

  • Mark all 'O's that are connected to the boundary as safe.
  • At the end, flip all other 'O's (unvisited ones) into 'X'.

Approach

  1. Use DFS (or BFS) starting from boundary cells. Whenever we see an 'O' on the boundary, perform DFS/BFS to mark all connected 'O's as visited (safe).
  2. After this traversal, all boundary-connected 'O's remain as they are, because they cannot be surrounded.
  3. Traverse the entire matrix:
    • If an 'O' is visited, leave it as 'O'.
    • If an 'O' is not visited, convert it into 'X'.
Code
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253545556575859606162636465666768697071727374757677787980818283848586
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
private:
    // DFS to mark boundary-connected 'O's as visited
    void dfs(int row, int col, vector<vector<int>>& vis, vector<vector<char>>& mat, int delrow[], int delcol[]) {
        // mark current cell visited
        vis[row][col] = 1;
        // cache dimensions
        int n = mat.size(), m = mat[0].size();
        // try 4 directions
        for (int k = 0; k < 4; k++) {
            // compute next cell
            int nrow = row + delrow[k], ncol = col + delcol[k];
            // check bounds and unvisited 'O'
            if (nrow >= 0 && nrow < n && ncol >= 0 && ncol < m && !vis[nrow][ncol] && mat[nrow][ncol] == 'O') {
                // continue DFS
                dfs(nrow, ncol, vis, mat, delrow, delcol);
            }
        }
    }

public:
    // Flip all 'O' not connected to boundary to 'X'
    vector<vector<char>> fill(int n, int m, vector<vector<char>> mat) {
        // handle empty matrix
        if (n == 0 || m == 0) return mat;
        // direction arrays for 4-neighbors
        int delrow[4] = {-1, 0, 1, 0};
        int delcol[4] = {0, 1, 0, -1};
        // visited matrix
        vector<vector<int>> vis(n, vector<int>(m, 0));

        // traverse first and last row
        for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
            // start DFS from unvisited boundary 'O' (top row)
            if (!vis[0][j] && mat[0][j] == 'O') dfs(0, j, vis, mat, delrow, delcol);
            // start DFS from unvisited boundary 'O' (bottom row)
            if (!vis[n - 1][j] && mat[n - 1][j] == 'O') dfs(n - 1, j, vis, mat, delrow, delcol);
        }

        // traverse first and last column
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            // start DFS from unvisited boundary 'O' (left col)
            if (!vis[i][0] && mat[i][0] == 'O') dfs(i, 0, vis, mat, delrow, delcol);
            // start DFS from unvisited boundary 'O' (right col)
            if (!vis[i][m - 1] && mat[i][m - 1] == 'O') dfs(i, m - 1, vis, mat, delrow, delcol);
        }

        // flip all unvisited 'O' to 'X'
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                // convert enclosed 'O' to 'X'
                if (!vis[i][j] && mat[i][j] == 'O') mat[i][j] = 'X';
            }
        }

        // return updated board
        return mat;
    }
};

int main() {
    // sample board
    vector<vector<char>> mat{
        {'X','X','X','X'},
        {'X','O','X','X'},
        {'X','O','O','X'},
        {'X','O','X','X'},
        {'X','X','O','O'}
    };
    // create solver
    Solution ob;
    // compute result
    vector<vector<char>> ans = ob.fill((int)mat.size(), (int)mat[0].size(), mat);
    // print board
    for (int i = 0; i < (int)ans.size(); i++) {
        for (int j = 0; j < (int)ans[0].size(); j++) {
            cout << ans[i][j] << " ";
        }
        cout << "\n";
    }
    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778798081828384
import java.util.*;

class Solution {
    // DFS to mark boundary-connected 'O's as visited
    private void dfs(int r, int c, int[][] vis, char[][] mat, int[] dr, int[] dc) {
        // mark current cell visited
        vis[r][c] = 1;
        // cache dimensions
        int n = mat.length, m = mat[0].length;
        // try 4 directions
        for (int k = 0; k < 4; k++) {
            // compute next cell
            int nr = r + dr[k], nc = c + dc[k];
            // check bounds and unvisited 'O'
            if (nr >= 0 && nr < n && nc >= 0 && nc < m && vis[nr][nc] == 0 && mat[nr][nc] == 'O') {
                // continue DFS
                dfs(nr, nc, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            }
        }
    }

    // Flip all 'O' not connected to boundary to 'X'
    public char[][] fill(int n, int m, char[][] mat) {
        // handle empty matrix
        if (n == 0 || m == 0) return mat;
        // direction arrays
        int[] dr = {-1, 0, 1, 0};
        int[] dc = {0, 1, 0, -1};
        // visited matrix
        int[][] vis = new int[n][m];

        // traverse first and last row
        for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
            // DFS from top boundary 'O'
            if (vis[0][j] == 0 && mat[0][j] == 'O') dfs(0, j, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            // DFS from bottom boundary 'O'
            if (vis[n - 1][j] == 0 && mat[n - 1][j] == 'O') dfs(n - 1, j, vis, mat, dr, dc);
        }

        // traverse first and last column
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            // DFS from left boundary 'O'
            if (vis[i][0] == 0 && mat[i][0] == 'O') dfs(i, 0, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            // DFS from right boundary 'O'
            if (vis[i][m - 1] == 0 && mat[i][m - 1] == 'O') dfs(i, m - 1, vis, mat, dr, dc);
        }

        // flip all unvisited 'O' to 'X'
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                // convert enclosed 'O' to 'X'
                if (vis[i][j] == 0 && mat[i][j] == 'O') mat[i][j] = 'X';
            }
        }

        // return updated board
        return mat;
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        // sample board
        char[][] mat = {
            {'X','X','X','X'},
            {'X','O','X','X'},
            {'X','O','O','X'},
            {'X','O','X','X'},
            {'X','X','O','O'}
        };
        // create solver
        Solution ob = new Solution();
        // compute result
        char[][] ans = ob.fill(mat.length, mat[0].length, mat);
        // print board
        for (int i = 0; i < ans.length; i++) {
            for (int j = 0; j < ans[0].length; j++) {
                System.out.print(ans[i][j] + " ");
            }
            System.out.println();
        }
    }
}

class Solution:
    # DFS to mark boundary-connected 'O's as visited
    def dfs(self, r, c, vis, mat, dr, dc):
        # mark current cell visited
        vis[r][c] = 1
        # cache dimensions
        n, m = len(mat), len(mat[0])
        # try 4 directions
        for k in range(4):
            # compute next cell
            nr, nc = r + dr[k], c + dc[k]
            # check bounds and unvisited 'O'
            if 0 <= nr < n and 0 <= nc < m and vis[nr][nc] == 0 and mat[nr][nc] == 'O':
                # continue DFS
                self.dfs(nr, nc, vis, mat, dr, dc)

    # Flip all 'O' not connected to boundary to 'X'
    def fill(self, n, m, mat):
        # handle empty matrix
        if n == 0 or m == 0:
            return mat
        # direction arrays
        dr, dc = [-1, 0, 1, 0], [0, 1, 0, -1]
        # visited matrix
        vis = [[0] * m for _ in range(n)]

        # traverse first and last row
        for j in range(m):
            # DFS from top boundary 'O'
            if vis[0][j] == 0 and mat[0][j] == 'O':
                self.dfs(0, j, vis, mat, dr, dc)
            # DFS from bottom boundary 'O'
            if vis[n - 1][j] == 0 and mat[n - 1][j] == 'O':
                self.dfs(n - 1, j, vis, mat, dr, dc)

        # traverse first and last column
        for i in range(n):
            # DFS from left boundary 'O'
            if vis[i][0] == 0 and mat[i][0] == 'O':
                self.dfs(i, 0, vis, mat, dr, dc)
            # DFS from right boundary 'O'
            if vis[i][m - 1] == 0 and mat[i][m - 1] == 'O':
                self.dfs(i, m - 1, vis, mat, dr, dc)

        # flip all unvisited 'O' to 'X'
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(m):
                # convert enclosed 'O' to 'X'
                if vis[i][j] == 0 and mat[i][j] == 'O':
                    mat[i][j] = 'X'

        # return updated board
        return mat


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # sample board
    mat = [
        ['X','X','X','X'],
        ['X','O','X','X'],
        ['X','O','O','X'],
        ['X','O','X','X'],
        ['X','X','O','O']
    ]
    # create solver
    ob = Solution()
    # compute result
    ans = ob.fill(len(mat), len(mat[0]), mat)
    # print board
    for row in ans:
        print(*row)

class Solution {
    // DFS to mark boundary-connected 'O's as visited
    dfs(r, c, vis, mat, dr, dc) {
        // mark current cell visited
        vis[r][c] = 1;
        // cache dimensions
        const n = mat.length, m = mat[0].length;
        // try 4 directions
        for (let k = 0; k < 4; k++) {
            // compute next cell
            const nr = r + dr[k], nc = c + dc[k];
            // check bounds and unvisited 'O'
            if (nr >= 0 && nr < n && nc >= 0 && nc < m && vis[nr][nc] === 0 && mat[nr][nc] === 'O') {
                // continue DFS
                this.dfs(nr, nc, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            }
        }
    }

    // Flip all 'O' not connected to boundary to 'X'
    fill(n, m, mat) {
        // handle empty matrix
        if (n === 0 || m === 0) return mat;
        // direction arrays
        const dr = [-1, 0, 1, 0], dc = [0, 1, 0, -1];
        // visited matrix
        const vis = Array.from({ length: n }, () => Array(m).fill(0));

        // traverse first and last row
        for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
            // DFS from top boundary 'O'
            if (vis[0][j] === 0 && mat[0][j] === 'O') this.dfs(0, j, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            // DFS from bottom boundary 'O'
            if (vis[n - 1][j] === 0 && mat[n - 1][j] === 'O') this.dfs(n - 1, j, vis, mat, dr, dc);
        }

        // traverse first and last column
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            // DFS from left boundary 'O'
            if (vis[i][0] === 0 && mat[i][0] === 'O') this.dfs(i, 0, vis, mat, dr, dc);
            // DFS from right boundary 'O'
            if (vis[i][m - 1] === 0 && mat[i][m - 1] === 'O') this.dfs(i, m - 1, vis, mat, dr, dc);
        }

        // flip all unvisited 'O' to 'X'
        for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            for (let j = 0; j < m; j++) {
                // convert enclosed 'O' to 'X'
                if (vis[i][j] === 0 && mat[i][j] === 'O') mat[i][j] = 'X';
            }
        }

        // return updated board
        return mat;
    }
}

// Driver code
(function () {
    // sample board
    const mat = [
        ['X','X','X','X'],
        ['X','O','X','X'],
        ['X','O','O','X'],
        ['X','O','X','X'],
        ['X','X','O','O']
    ];
    // create solver
    const ob = new Solution();
    // compute result
    const ans = ob.fill(mat.length, mat[0].length, mat);
    // print board
    for (const row of ans) {
        console.log(row.join(' '));
    }
})();
Complexity Analysis

Time Complexity: O(N × M), since each cell is visited at most once during DFS and once during the final traversal.

Space Complexity: O(N × M), due to the visited matrix and the recursion stack in the worst case.
